title: Executive Order 12039—Science and Technology Policy Functions

flags.justification: Functions are distributed across the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy, the Director of OMB, and the Director of the National Science Foundation, each with specific carve-outs.
Clear, specific, statute-anchored transfers implementing a named Reorganization Plan.
Multiple related transfers all coherently implement one reorganization plan.
An internal reorganization order, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing reorganization of an ongoing government function.
This implements a congressionally-approved reorganization plan under the Reorganization Act's own procedures, a legitimate, sanctioned restructuring rather than a removal of oversight.
An internal reorganization with no third-party compliance regime.
Establishes the lasting institutional structure for federal science-policy coordination, building on the framework begun in EO 10521.
A careful, statute-anchored division of roles across multiple agencies with specific carve-outs avoids overlap despite the reorganization's complexity.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. Implements Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1977, restructuring federal science-policy coordination following on from EO 10521's foundational framework.
